briefdescription.para: This function setup SI5324 clock generator either in free or locked mode.

parameterdescription.para: specifies an index for selecting mode frequency.
specifies either free or locked mode.

listitem.para: Zero if error in programming external clock.

briefdescription.para: This function outputs the video timing , Audio, Link Status, HDMI RX state of HDMI RX core.

detaileddescription.para: In addition, it also prints information about HDMI TX, and HDMI GT cores.

briefdescription.para: This function prints additional details of the system for debug purpose.
This function is called whenever an error condition in VPHY occurs.

detaileddescription.para: This will fill the FIFO of VPHY error events which will be processed outside the ISR.

parameterdescription.para: is the VPHY instance pointer
is the VPHY error type

briefdescription.para: This function is called in the application to process the pending VPHY errors.

simplesect.para: This function can be expanded to perform necessary actions depending on the error type. For example, XHDMIPHY1_ERR_PLL_LAYOUT can be used to automatically switch in and out of bonded mode for GTXE2 devices

briefdescription.para: This function is used to initialize the system timer that is used to count and generate given periodic interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the example design handler
is the device id of the system timer
is the interrupt vector id of the system timer

briefdescription.para: This function time specified in Us to ticks for a specified timer frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is the time in microseconds.
is the frequency of the clock to be used for counting the ticks.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to start a the timer to generate periodic pulses of tmrctr interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the example design handler.
is the user specified period for the timer pulses.

briefdescription.para: This function is set as the callback handler from the timer counter interrupt handler.

detaileddescription.para: The counters that are used in the application for timer based control and exclusion are be handled in the this function.
The user needs to modify this callback to set the counters and exclusion flags based on the pulse counts in the pulse train generated by the system timer.

parameterdescription.para: is the function callback reference.
is the timer counter number.

briefdescription.para: This function is set as the callback handler from the interrupt controller for the system timer interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable and additional debugging prints in the application.

parameterdescription.para: is the function to enable printing.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable and additional debugging prints for the Aux Fifo operations in the application.

parameterdescription.para: is the function to enable printing.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable and additional debugging prints for the HDCP operations in the application.

parameterdescription.para: is the function to enable printing.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to initialize the example design controller data structure that is used to track the presence and instances of the hdmi 2.1 receiver and transmitter.

parameterdescription.para: is the instance of the example design handle data structure.

briefdescription.para: This function updates the global array for AUX fifo with the AUX data from the hdmi receiver.
This function updates the Reads the VRR Video Timing Values from HDMI RX Receiver.
This function updates the Reads the VRR Video Timing Values from HDMI RX Receiver and updates to HDMI TX when VRR Manual Mode is enabled.
This function updates the Reads the VRR VTEM Packet from HDMI RX Receiver.
This function updates the R Reads the VRR VTEM Packet from HDMI RX Receiver and updates to HDMI TX.
This function checks if there has been a change in resolution on the incoming rx stream.
This function setup sets up the On Board IIC MUX to select device.
This is the callback for assertion error.

parameterdescription.para: is string name of the file where the assertion failure occurred.
is the line wheer the assertion failure occurred.

briefdescription.para: This function updates the FrameRate for the current video stream.

parameterdescription.para: is the device id of the ps iic device.
is the device id of the ps iic device.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the clock source is successfully set. XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function setups the interrupt system so interrupts can occur for the HDMI cores.

detaileddescription.para: The function is application-specific since the actual system may or may not have an interrupt controller. The HDMI cores could be directly connected to a processor without an interrupt controller. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if interrupt setup was successful.
A specific error code defined in "xstatus.h" if an error occurs.

simplesect.para: This function assumes a Microblaze system and no operating system is used.

briefdescription.para: Main function to call example with HDMI TX, HDMI RX and HDMI GT drivers.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if HDMI example was successfully.

inbodydescription.para: Setup IIC devices and clock sources.
Initialize the HDMI Video Transmitter "state machine" functionality and the downstream HDCP device.
Initialize the HDMI Video Receiver "state machine" functionality and the downstream HDCP device.
Set the Video Phy Error callbacks.
Initialize the controller for the example design mode.
Initialize the TPG and associated GPIO to reset the TPG.

briefdescription.para: This file demonstrates how to use Xilinx HDMI TX Subsystem, HDMI RX Subsystem and Video PHY Controller drivers.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes dd/mm/yy
1.00 YB 11/05/19 Initial release. 1.01 KU 07/07/20 Support for FrameBuffer Can use FRL on RX and TMDS on TX Video will be clipped if RX resolution in more than 4K Support Revision 2 (Pass 4) of OnSemi retimer Menu option updated for EDID and Training based on MaxRate configuration Added support for 16 BPC 1.02 KU 30/11/20 AVI InfoFrame Version set to 3 for resolutions VIC > 127 Onsemi redriver tweaked for TMDS mode in TX 1.03 ssh 03/17/21 Added EdidHdmi20_t, PsIic0 and PsIic1 declarations 1.04 ssh 04/20/21 Added support for Dynamic HDR and Versal 1.05 ssh 07/14/21 Added support for Native Video 1.06 KU 30/08/21 RX FRL settings updated for VCU118 1.07 ssh 01/28/22 Updated GT Swing settings for VCK190 1.08 ssh 02/01/22 Updated Enable CTS Conversion Function 1.09 ssh 02/04/22 Added param to support Tx to train at the same rate as Rx 1.10 ssh 03/02/22 Added LCPLL and RPLL config for VCK190 Exdes 1.11 ssh 01/25/23 Added support for VEK280 1.12 ssh 09/14/23 Added Tx compliance values for UltrascalePlus 1.13 ssh 04/07/25 Added support for VEK385
